# Test: Git History and Authorship Preservation (US4)
# Validates that git subtree publishing preserves commit history and authorship.
# Creates multiple commits from different authors and verifies they're preserved.
#
# Prerequisites: k3d and Gitea running (handled by TestE2E)
# Environment: GITEA_URL, GITEA_TOKEN, E2E_CLUSTER are injected by test runner

# Step 1: Create app repository
mkdir app
cd app
exec git init
exec git config user.name 'Author One'
exec git config user.email 'author1@example.com'
exec apx init app --org=testorg --repo=test-app --non-interactive internal/apis/proto/history/v1
exists internal/apis/proto/history/v1/history.proto

# Commit 1: Initial schema by Author One
exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Initial history schema by author one'

# Commit 2: Update by Author Two
exec git config user.name 'Author Two'
exec git config user.email 'author2@example.com'

exec sh -c 'echo "// Updated by Author Two" >> internal/apis/proto/history/v1/history.proto'
exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Add comment by author two'

# Commit 3: Another update by Author Three
exec git config user.name 'Author Three'
exec git config user.email 'author3@example.com'

exec sh -c 'echo "// Updated by Author Three" >> internal/apis/proto/history/v1/history.proto'
exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Add comment by author three'

# Commit 4: Update by Author One again
exec git config user.name 'Author One'
exec git config user.email 'author1@example.com'

exec sh -c 'echo "// Second update by Author One" >> internal/apis/proto/history/v1/history.proto'
exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Second update by author one'

# Commit 5: Final update by Author Two
exec git config user.name 'Author Two'
exec git config user.email 'author2@example.com'

exec sh -c 'echo "// Final update by Author Two" >> internal/apis/proto/history/v1/history.proto'
exec git add .
exec git commit -m 'Final update by author two'

exec git tag proto/history/v1/v1.0.0

# Step 2: Verify all 5 commits exist
exec git log --oneline
stdout 'Initial history schema by author one'
stdout 'Add comment by author two'
stdout 'Add comment by author three'
stdout 'Second update by author one'
stdout 'Final update by author two'

# Step 3: Verify all 3 authors are in git log
exec git log --format='%an'
stdout 'Author One'
stdout 'Author Two'
stdout 'Author Three'

# Step 4: Verify author emails
exec git log --format='%ae'
stdout 'author1@example.com'
stdout 'author2@example.com'
stdout 'author3@example.com'

# Step 5: Verify commit count is exactly 5
exec git rev-list --count HEAD
stdout '5'

# Step 6: Add an unrelated file (not in schema directory) for commit filtering test
exec sh -c 'echo "# README" > README.md'
exec git add README.md
exec git commit -m 'Unrelated README change'

# Now we have 6 commits total but only 5 are schema-related
exec git rev-list --count HEAD
stdout '6'

# Step 7: Verify publish dry-run works with full history
exec apx publish --module-path=internal/apis/proto/history/v1 --canonical-repo=github.com/testorg/history-test --dry-run
stdout 'Dry-run mode'

cd ..

# Success: Git history and authorship preservation validated
# - 5 schema-related commits from 3 different authors
# - All commit messages preserved
# - All author names and emails preserved
# - Unrelated commits exist but should be filtered during subtree publish
